strengths: Effective self-management strategies, including mobile reminders and use of a Pomodoro-style app, show strong personal regulation.
Comprehensive information-literacy demonstrated by thorough secondary research, well-structured research-log tables, and accurate referencing.
Analytical skills evidenced by a clear solution-analysis table and ACCESS FM evaluation of existing cookbook layouts.
Primary research skills highlighted by a detailed interview transcript and synthesis of insights.
Reflective decision-making in narrowing recipe choices ensures accessibility and user focus.
weaknesses: The impact of each ATL skill on the final productâs quality is not explicitly explained.
Connections between specific ATL strategies and measurable improvements or outcomes in the product could be strengthened.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The student applies a wide range of ATL skillsâresearch, analysis, self-management, and media literacyâwith substantial supporting evidence. To deepen this section, they should explicitly link each applied ATL skill to its effect on the productâs development and quality, using metrics or comparative examples to illustrate effectiveness.

strengths: Concluding statement reinforces focus on how success criteria will guide the work, demonstrating clear purpose.
Articulation of the objective effectively links personal passion for cooking and product design.
Inclusion of a personal backstory (grandmother) provides strong purpose and relevance to the cookbook project.
weaknesses: Learning goal combines multiple aims and lacks specificity and measurable targets.
Success criteria such as âeducational valueâ and âaesthetic appealâ lack concrete benchmarks or metrics.
Action plan omits detailed resource requirements, contingency steps for internet-dependent tasks, deadlines, sequencing, and evidence of interim progress.
Rationale does not specify how data will be collected or analyzed to assess each criterion.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The planning section establishes a compelling personal motivation and clear overarching objectives, showing strong alignment with the intended product. To make the plan fully actionable and assessable, the student should refine the learning goal into discrete, measurable targets; define specific metrics for each success criterion; include detailed timelines, resource assignments, and contingency plans; and outline methods for gathering and analyzing data.

strengths: Insightful evaluation of skill growth, such as increased efficiency with design tools (Canva shortcuts).
Deep personal reflection on how the project influenced career aspirations and overall well-being.
Adaptability demonstrated by reflecting on task adjustments made in response to internet constraints.
Process improvement awareness shown through reflection on pre-writing recipes and post-research editing.
Sophisticated evaluative insight in recognizing the value of broader user feedback and more realistic timelines.
Clear evidence of enhanced time-management and discipline, and awareness of its impact on workflow.
weaknesses: Self-assigned grade rationale lacks direct linkage to specific feedback and rubric criteria levels.
Reflection would benefit from identifying any remaining challenges or areas still needing development to show a fully balanced perspective.
feedback.calculationMethod: Mini report holistic evaluation: The reflections are thoughtful and comprehensive, showcasing high self-awareness and clear examples of personal and process growth. To further strengthen this section, the student should anchor their self-evaluation in explicit references to rubric criteria and feedback data, and acknowledge any persistent challenges to demonstrate balanced, ongoing reflection.

annotations.comment: Your concluding statement concisely reinforces how the criteria will guide your workâgood focus on the planâs purpose.
Adopting a mobile screenshot for your action-plan reminders is a clever self-management strategyâgood evidence of personal regulation.
Your learning goal is descriptive but combines multiple aims. Refine it by defining specific, measurable criteria (e.g. âwrite five clear recipes following X structureâ) to make success tangible.
Listing criteria is good, but âeducational valueâ and âaesthetic appealâ need specific targets or metrics so you can measure success objectively.
Your action plan lists tasks and reflections but omits clear resource needs and contingency steps for internet-dependent tasks; consider adding backup resources.
You clearly articulate the objective to develop instructive writing and user-friendly design, effectively linking your passion for cooking and product design.
The personal backstory about your grandmother gives strong purpose and relevance to your cookbook product.
Your rationale describes why each criterion matters but doesnât specify how youâll collect or analyze data (e.g. survey questions, scoring rubric) to assess performance.
The success-criteria table is comprehensive yet lacks deadlines, resource assignments and step sequencing to show how youâll achieve each level.
Noting increased efficiency with Canva shortcuts shows youâve critically evaluated your skill growth in design tools.
Later entries in your action plan lack completion evidence and justification for changes; include interim check-ins and updated timelines.
Excellent description of your secondary research processâusing multiple sources, accurate referencing, and synthesizing findings shows strong information-literacy skills.
Your solution-analysis table clearly applies design criteria (Aesthetics, Function, etc.)âsolid evidence of applying analytical skills to product design.
Your research-log table is well structured, providing clear evidence of findings and summariesâstrong application of researchâskill organization.
Great use of ACCESS FM analysis on existing cookbook layoutsâthis shows critical media-literacy and user-perspective evaluation.
Including a detailed interview transcript demonstrates robust media-literacy and primary-research application; strong evidence for ATL skills.
Your reflective process in narrowing recipe choices shows thoughtful use of self-management and reflective ATL skills to ensure accessibility.
Using the Pomodoro-style Study Bunny app shows effective affective self-management and focus strategiesâexcellent application of ATL skills.
Your evaluation of how this project changed your career aspirations reveals deep personal growth and genuine impact on learning.
Your reflection on adapting tasks due to internet constraints demonstrates high self-awareness and flexibilityâstrong reflective insight.
Discussing how you pre-wrote recipes and then edited them post-research shows proactive reflection on process improvement.
Identifying the value of broader user feedback (questionnaires) and more realistic timelines shows sophisticated evaluative reflection.
Reflecting on improved time-management and discipline illustrates clear evidence of skills development and its effect on your wellbeing.
Your self-assigned grade rationale would be stronger with direct links to specific feedback data (e.g. survey results) and criteria levels.
